scenarios.title: Early-stage request routes to office-hours
scenarios.prompt: Autoplan this feature idea. We think we need a partner dashboard, but we have not pinned down the user pain yet.
scenarios.expected_without_skill: Treats the request like a normal plan review even though the request is still at the idea stage.
scenarios.expected_with_skill: Recognizes that planning maturity is low, routes to `codex-office-hours`, and returns a consolidated brief plus next step.

scenarios.title: Existing plan routes to plan review
scenarios.prompt: Autoplan this. We already have a plan doc for API key rotation and admin audit screens. I want the gaps closed before implementation.
scenarios.expected_without_skill: Repeats generic planning advice and does not choose a concrete review path.
scenarios.expected_with_skill: Detects that a plan already exists, routes to `codex-plan-review`, and returns a single revised-plan oriented outcome.

scenarios.title: Autoplan should acknowledge mode before interactive planning
scenarios.prompt: Autoplan this in Default mode. I want you to choose the right planning path and keep going.
scenarios.expected_without_skill: Pretends it can switch collaboration modes automatically and continue with an interactive planning flow.
scenarios.expected_with_skill: Chooses the right planning route, tells the user to enter Plan Mode with Shift+Tab for the interactive path, and still leaves a compact consolidated planning draft.

scenarios.title: Execution-ready request should not be shoved into planning
scenarios.prompt: Autoplan this. The feature is already implemented, so just tell me the right next execution step and do not send me into a planning loop.
scenarios.expected_without_skill: Still routes into office-hours or plan-review because the word autoplan was used, even though the work is already execution-ready.
scenarios.expected_with_skill: Classifies the request as execution-ready, says the planning pack is not applicable, keeps the user in Default mode, and points to implementation or review execution as the next step.

scenarios.title: Explicit planning skill choice should win
scenarios.prompt: Use codex-plan-review on this rough implementation plan. Do not send me to office-hours.
scenarios.expected_without_skill: Second-guesses the user's explicit skill choice and reroutes based on vague heuristics.
scenarios.expected_with_skill: Respects the explicit planning skill choice, keeps the route on codex-plan-review, and only applies mode-aware guidance around Shift+Tab if needed.
